title: oenothera fruticosa subsp. tetragona
intro: Oenothera fruticosa subsp. tetragona, also known as Four-leaved Evening Primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Oenothera fruticosa subsp. tetragona include potential relief from anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties, as well as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may contribute to cardiovascular and skin health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be made into ointments and salves for topical application.
